items.how_used: 论文列出了9条明确的假设，包括ISS用途、年补给量、各国飞船频率、NASA宇航员数量等，为后续建模奠定基础。
items.source_excerpt:
Assumptions
1. The ISS is maintained for the purpose of scientific research.
2. The ISS will require 26,776 kg of supplies per year.
3. Russia will send 4 shuttles per year...
4. ...
5. 4 NASA astronauts will be sent to the ISS per year.
6. ...
7. The number of astronauts in NASA stays constant at 64.
8. ...
9. The Dragon C and Cygnus shuttles will both be ready for use in 2012.

items.how_used: 通过平均历史数据预测俄罗斯和日本未来的年补给量，使用简单平均法（线性回归的特例）。
items.source_excerpt: Russia’s future supply value per year was found by averaging the supply mass sent by Russia each year from 2008 to 2011. This comes out to a value of 10,152 kg per year.

items.how_used: 对欧洲的补给量，论文选择使用最近一年（2011）的数据作为未来预测，而非平均，这是一种基于趋势的判断。
items.source_excerpt: Europe’s future supply value per year was found differently. Instead of averaging Europe’s two very different supply values, 2,297 kg and 7,084 kg in 2008 and 2011 respectively, we decided that the 2011 supply mass value (7,084 kg) was more representative of what Europe will be able to send in the future.

items.how_used: 将ISS维护总成本分解为人员、运营、研究和补给四个部分，分别计算后求和，得到总成本模型。
items.source_excerpt: Thorough research reveals that NASA’s operations and expenses related to the ISS can be divided into 4 broad categories: I. Personnel, II. Operation, III. Research, IV. Resupply.

items.how_used: 论文未明确进行灵敏度分析，但通过假设不同情景（如商业飞船延迟）来讨论计划的可调整性。
items.source_excerpt: Another option would be for NASA to develop its own manned spacecraft to replace the US Space Shuttle Program. A good candidate for this is the Orion Multi-Purpose Crew Vehicle (Orion MPCV).

items.how_used: 引言部分清晰陈述了问题背景（航天飞机退役）、目标（制定十年计划）和模型框架（四个成本类别）。
items.source_excerpt: On July 21, 2011, the 135th and final US Space Shuttle arrived back on Earth, marking the end of the US Space Shuttle program. ... Our group will develop a comprehensive plan that outlines the costs, payloads, and flight schedules NASA will have to field over the next ten years (2012 – 2021) in order to properly maintain the ISS.

items.how_used: 论文在变量定义部分列出了所有关键符号及其含义，便于后续公式引用。
items.source_excerpt:
Definition of Variables
t = Time (years)
M = Total ISS maintenance cost (USD)
R = Non-transport Research Expenses (USD)
Q = Cost per kilogram of Payload
c = Cost per year of Maintaining Crew Members
s = Price per Seat
A = Total Cost per Year from Astronaut Salary (USD)
r = Average Astronaut Salary (USD)
G = Total Ground Operation Expenses (millions of USD)
E = Total cost of Transporting Research Material (USD)
m = Total mass transported (kg)
Z = Total Cost of Research (USD)

items.how_used: 论文使用了表格（Table 1, Table 2）来展示历史数据和预测数据，清晰直观。
items.source_excerpt:
Table 1: Mass of Supplies (kg) Transported to the ISS by Contributing Entity per Year
2008 2009 2010 2011
Russia 10,704 9,352 10,203 10,348
Europe 2297 0 0 7084
Japan 0 4082 0 4808
USA 4,536 4,536 4,536 4,536
TOTAL 13,001 13,434 10,203 26,776
